what_it_does: TypeScript declaration file for a Tabs React component. Declares a TabItem interface ({id?, label}), a TabsProps interface extending HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> (with onChange omitted/redefined), and the Tabs function plus default export.
observations: TabsProps.items accepts an Array of TabItem OR string (string shorthand allowed)
value and onChange are optional, making the component controllable but not requiring it
onChange signature is (id: string, e: React.MouseEvent) => void — passes both the tab id and the click event
Uses Omit<HTMLAttributes, 'onChange'> to replace the native onChange with the custom one
JSDoc comment describes it as a 'Dashboard tab bar' where the active tab gets a 'gold underline'
standouts: The JSDoc ties the type to a specific visual treatment ('gold underline'), embedding design intent in the type declaration
conventions: Standard React .d.ts pattern: import * as React, named export + default export of the same component
notes: Type contract for a controlled Tabs component; items can be strings or {id,label}, active tab gets a gold underline.
questions: What is the default/uncontrolled behavior when value is omitted — does the .jsx track internal state?
When items are strings, what becomes the id used in onChange?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — declaration file; the JSDoc 'Dashboard tab bar' purpose matches the declared shape.
apparent_themes: design intent encoded in types (gold underline)
intention_signals: Aims to give consumers a typed, controllable tab bar with minimal-boilerplate input (string shorthand) while exposing the click event for richer handling.
novelty: Embedding a concrete visual treatment ('gold underline') directly in the type's JSDoc.
contribution: Contributes the typed public contract for the Tabs component — the string|object item flexibility and the (id, event) onChange signature a fused system would keep for tab interaction.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript declaration for a controlled React Tabs component with TabItem/TabsProps interfaces, string-or-object items, and an (id, MouseEvent) onChange.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the typed public API for a dashboard tab bar component with a gold-underline active state.
